title: pilosella glomerata subsp. calomelana
intro: Pilosella glomerata subsp. calomelana is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant in the dandelion family, characterized by its hairy leaves and yellow flowers. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been attributed to the presence of sesquiterpene lactones and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Pilosella glomerata subsp. calomelana include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these effects. Herbal preparations of this plant are typically made by infusing the leaves in hot water or using them in a decoction, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any herbal remedy.
